repo_prior_art.cite: none; doc/API.md §3 says ThmSmith PartialID is empty and Q1 panel entries are orthogonal

published_prior_art.cite: DoudchenkoImbens2016 allows negative/signed weights; exact box-support multiplier not stated

repo_prior_art.cite: none; doc/API.md §3 says ThmSmith PartialID is empty and Q1 panel entries are orthogonal

published_prior_art.cite: NazaretShiBlei2024 gives misspecification intervals/bounds for SC, but not this signed fixed-weight support-function bridge interval

repo_prior_art.cite: none; no archived same-qid proposal found in live doc/formalization and doc/API.md §3 PartialID is empty

published_prior_art.cite: BenMichaelFellerRothstein2021 and KelloggMogstadPouliotTorgovitsky2021 already frame negative/extrapolating SC weights as an extrapolation-bias problem; sharp partial-ID radii are not stated

repo_prior_art.cite: none; Q1_partial_did_unobserved_trends concerns panel pretrend summaries, not SC weight/bridge leverage

published_prior_art.cite: FirpoPossebom2018 and ChernozhukovWuthrichZhu2021 use SC test/statistical summaries; deterministic nonsharpness of RMSPE-only partial-ID summaries is adjacent rather than subsumed

novelty_flags.label: Conjecture 1
novelty_flags.one_line: Misses KelloggMogstadPouliotTorgovitsky2021, a close JASA comparator on extrapolation/interpolation bias in SC; without engaging it, tier=letter below novelty_target=field.

soundness_flags.label: Conjecture 1
soundness_flags.one_line: The phrase 'same exact-factor point prediction' is not formally defined, and Assumption prefit-pair writes S_s(w^c)=S_s(w^x) 'under exact factor fit' although S_s is an observed donor-weighted post outcome.

soundness_flags.label: Conjecture 2
soundness_flags.one_line: 'Scalar sensitivity rule' is under-specified: the statement must define whether the rule returns a radius or interval and which inputs besides RMSPE/||g|| and eta are forbidden.

strengthening_paths.suggestion: Add Kellogg, Mogstad, Pouliot, and Torgovitsky (2021, JASA) as a named comparator and explain why a sharp bounded-error interval is not just their extrapolation-bias tradeoff in partial-ID language.

Promote Conjecture 1 from a generic pre-fit-equivalence statement to a quantitative open-set theorem with an explicit minimum radius gap in terms of affine-nullspace geometry and distance from the simplex.

Replace the oracle bridge with either an estimated/identified bridge class or a joint interval over plausible a_s; that is the most plausible route from letter-tier support-function algebra to field-tier methodology.

recommended_next_step: Revise the proposal around a quantified open-set separation and a sharper comparison to extrapolation-bias SC papers before sending to Stage 0.
